sections.title: Measurement object
sections.body: RIASEC explains tendencies in work-activity interests. It does not measure ability, personality identity, career values, mental state, income potential, or probability of career success.

sections.title: Score space
sections.body: 0-100 is a within-form interest-strength conversion. It is not a population percentile and should not be used to compare raw score movement across 60Q and 140Q.

sections.title: 60Q and 140Q
sections.body: 60Q describes the basic interest structure. 140Q describes more contextual clues from tasks, environments, and role responsibilities. 140Q is more specific, not more accurate.

sections.title: Occupation example boundary
sections.body: Occupation examples must be mediated by activity and task explanations. They are content examples, not matches, recommendations, rankings, or job-fit decisions.

sections.title: Validation status
sections.body: When external validity evidence is not attached, use theory_based, under_validation, or internally_pilot_required. Do not claim externally_validated status.

sections.title: Feedback boundary
sections.body: Saved, excluded, aspiration, and disagreement feedback only affects exploration paths. It does not change the measured Holland Code, scores, or report snapshot.

sections.title: Privacy and use
sections.body: Share and PDF surfaces do not expose raw feedback, life stage, organization context, or internal snapshot_id by default.

sections.title: Restricted use
sections.body: This report must not be used for recruiting screening, admission, promotion, elimination, qualification decisions, medical diagnosis, or psychological diagnosis.

v5_editorial_note: English professional boundary draft keeps prohibited terms only in negative constraint contexts.
